verses.text: An altar of shittim wood for incense — one by one by two cubits, horns of one piece. Overlay pure gold; gold crown, rings, staves. Set before the vail by the ark. Aaron burns sweet incense morning and evening, perpetual before the LORD. No strange incense, no other offering. Yearly, atone its horns with sin offering blood — most holy. Guidance: The golden altar stands before the vail. Incense pictures prayer rising morning and evening. Christ is our intercessor here; Revelation says our prayers reach this altar.

When thou takest the sum of Israel, every man give a ransom of his soul to the LORD — that no plague befall in numbering. Half a shekel by the sanctuary shekel — rich gives no more, poor no less. Atonement money for the tabernacle, memorial before the LORD. Guidance: Every life costs the same — half a shekel. God values rich and poor at the identical price. The ransom funds the tabernacle and remembers each soul before Him.

Make a brass laver and foot for washing; set between the tabernacle and altar with water. Aaron and his sons wash their hands and feet there. When they go in to minister or come near the altar, they shall wash — that they die not. A statute forever throughout generations. Guidance: Hands and feet washed before service. Even consecrated priests need daily cleansing. Jesus tells Peter: he who has bathed needs only to wash his feet — covenant union, then daily walk.

Principal spices — myrrh 500, cinnamon 250, calamus 250, cassia 500, one hin olive oil. Compound by the apothecary's art — holy anointing oil. Anoint tabernacle, ark, table, candlestick, altars, laver — most holy; what touches them is holy. Anoint Aaron and sons. Pour it not on common flesh; make none like it. Whoever copies or shares with a stranger is cut off. Guidance: One recipe, one purpose. The oil that anoints kings and priests cannot be sold or worn. What God sets apart stays His.

Sweet spices — stacte, onycha, galbanum, pure frankincense — equal weights. Make a perfume tempered together, pure, holy. Beat some small; put before the testimony where I meet thee — most holy. Make none like it for personal smell; holy to the LORD. Whoever copies it to smell shall be cut off. Guidance: The same rule for incense as for oil. God's perfume is not for anyone's wardrobe. Worship has its own scent, set apart from common pleasure.
